samples.name: incident-log-multi-entity
samples.description: The Phase-1 anchor artefact: an incident log line mixing customer email, IBAN, internal IP, a second email, a phone number, a sort code, and a rotation token on adjacent lines. Exercises multi-entity detection and stable placeholders.
samples.content:
2026-07-18T09:14:02Z ERROR customer jane.doe@example.com (acct GB29 NWBK 6016 1331 9268 19) failed login from 192.168.1.42
escalated to ops@example.org callback +44 20 7946 0958 sort code 12-34-56
session token Zx9Kq2Lm7Pw4Rt6Yv1Bn8Fs3Hd5Jc0Ga4We flagged for rotation

samples.name: yaml-hash-in-quoted-value
samples.description: T08 under-spanning reproducer #1: a `#` inside a quoted YAML value makes the parser mis-span the trailing text as a comment. Because detection scans the FULL raw buffer (spans are enrichment-only), the email after the `#` is still caught — the property T07 had to preserve.
samples.content:
incident:
  note: "customer #escalation contact a.smith@support.example.org urgent"
  replica_host: 10.0.0.5

samples.name: yaml-single-quoted-flow-scalar
samples.description: T08 under-spanning reproducer #2: a single-quoted YAML flow scalar the JSON-tokenizer fallback under-spans. Full-buffer detection still flags the high-entropy token inside it.

samples.name: dotfile-env-blocked
samples.description: A `.env` dotfile WITH its path hint — the artefact-level Block path. `mask` drops the whole content; detection still finds the secrets (recall is over detection, block is the extra guard). The bare-dotfile name is the regression that matters: `Path::new(".env").extension()` is None.
samples.content:
# production secrets - do not commit
export API_TOKEN=Zx9Kq2Lm7Pw4Rt6Yv1Bn8Fs3Hd5Jc0Ga4We
DB_PASSWORD=Kp7Zx2Lm9Qw4Rt6Yv1Bn8Cs3Hd5Jc0Ga4We
SMTP_USER=ops@example.org

samples.name: dotenv-shaped-no-path-hint
samples.description: The T09 known limit: dotenv-shaped content with NO path hint (a `Bash echo X > .env` bypasses the artefact Block). Only entity detection protects it here. The high-entropy token is caught; the short low-entropy password and the structured licence key are the residual that ONLY the artefact Block would have caught.

samples.name: json-tool-payload
samples.description: A Write-tool JSON payload whose masking must survive the v1.3 same_shape guard (string values only). Entities live inside the `content` string value; masking substitutes in place and the JSON stays shape-identical.

samples.name: overlapping-secret-email
samples.description: A high-entropy credential glued to an email by an `@` — one entropy `Secret` span overlaps one `Email` span over the same bytes. Exercises resolve_overlaps: the more-specific Email wins, no detected byte leaks.
samples.content: leaked credential Zx9Kq2Lm7Pw4Rt6Yv1Bn8Fs3Hd5Jc0Ga4We@ops.example.com found in the log

samples.name: benign-lookalikes
samples.description: Benign engineering text shaped like sensitive values: a git commit SHA (entropy look-alike), an ISBN and a zip+4 (phone look-alikes), an EMAIL_0015-style near-collision placeholder, a version string, an ISO date, and structured run-IDs/paths. Nothing here is sensitive — every finding is a false positive, measured per detector.
samples.content:
commit 17b27d5c8f9e2a1b3d4c5e6f7a8b9c0d1e2f3a4b merged on 2026-07-18
near-collision placeholder EMAIL_0015 referenced in ticket
ISBN 978-3-16-148410-0 and zip 12345-6789 in the shipping record
release v2.14.3 rolled out over 12 days
run-20260718-EG-012 completed; see scripts/gateway-run.sh for details

samples.name: display-collision-mint
samples.description: Display-collision (T09 doubt round): raw text contains the literal `EMAIL_001` AND a real email that the pack mints as `EMAIL_001`. On demask, the coincidental literal is also substituted — round-trip corruption. Incidence measured by mask->demask round-trip inequality.
samples.content: forward jane.doe@example.com to the team, and note the placeholder EMAIL_001 stays literal

samples.name: display-collision-spoof
samples.description: A spoofed `EMAIL_999` the pack never mints (only one email -> only EMAIL_001 minted). On demask, EMAIL_999 has no binding and is left untouched — validates that a placeholder-shaped string the pack never minted survives.
samples.content: forward jane.doe@example.com but ignore the spoofed marker EMAIL_999 in the body

samples.name: display-collision-no-mint
samples.description: Placeholder-shaped literals with NO real address present, so the pack mints nothing. Masking leaves the text unchanged and demask has no bindings — the literals survive. The 'without the pack minting the same display' half of the collision measurement.
samples.content: the doc literally mentions EMAIL_001 and EMAIL_002 as example placeholders, no real addresses here
